marketState: neutral, hot-weather bullish near-term vs storage-surplus/weak-seasonality bearish medium-term
summary: Front-month NG at $2.911 is trading almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.923, band $2.42-$3.42), so there is no valuation edge in either direction today. The dominant near-term driver is weather: 1-7 day and 8-16 day demand run +20.1% and +17.8% above the 5-year normal with range positions of 0.81 and 0.91, a clearly bullish cooling-demand signal that holds through the full 16-day forecast window. That bullish pull does not extend across the full 3-12 week horizon, and is offset there by a +180 Bcf storage surplus vs the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise (+22 Bcf vs forecast), weak/unreliable seasonality (price already -7.7pp below its typical-year path, only 38% odds of higher prices in 3-4 weeks, -1.4% typical 4-week move), daily/weekly technicals still bearish (EMA50<200, RSI ~38), and managed money net short at the 35th percentile. Net: lean long into near-term hot-weather strength, fade rallies further out where supply-side and seasonal signals dominate.

longTerm.entry: $3.05-$3.15 (rally into resistance near the Nov-2026 curve level $3.095 / plus21D fair $3.064)
longTerm.stop: $3.35 (above near-term curve resistance and the plus14D/21D fair-value drift)
longTerm.sizing: 0.5R approx 0.5% account risk (low conviction, Low seasonality reliability)
longTerm.rationale: Seasonality gives only 38% odds of higher prices in 3-4 weeks with a -1.4% typical move and price already running -7.7pp below its typical-year path (though reliability is flagged Low), storage sits +180 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish build surprise, daily/weekly technicals are bearish (EMA50<200, RSI ~38), and COT shows managed money net short (mmNet -60,295, 35th percentile) with room to add. The bullish weather signal only covers a 16-day window, a fraction of this 3-12 week horizon.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained close above $3.35, a storage deficit emerging vs the 5-year average, or seasonality/COT/weekly technicals flipping net-long or bullish would invalidate this view.

shortTerm.tp2: $3.12 (approaching Nov-contract curve level / start of long-term short zone)
shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D and 8-16D weather demand are running well above 5-year norms (+20.1%/+17.8%, range position 0.81/0.91), supporting spot strength into peak cooling season, and the front trades slightly above next (mild backwardation) consistent with prompt tightness. Price is fairly valued with room toward the rising plus7D/plus14D fair levels ($2.95-$3.00), and intraday technicals (5m/15m) show a bullish EMA/trend tilt even as higher timeframes stay weak.
shortTerm.invalidation: A sustained daily close below $2.80, or the 8-16D weather demand anomaly fading back toward the 5-year norm, would invalidate this view.

keyDrivers: Near/medium-term weather demand well above 5y norms (+20.1%/+17.8%, range position 0.81/0.91) - bullish near-term only
Storage surplus +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish build surprise (+22 Bcf vs forecast) - bearish medium-term
Seasonality: Low reliability, price already -7.7pp below typical-year path, only 38% odds of higher prices in 3-4 weeks
Daily/weekly technicals bearish (EMA50<200, RSI ~38) vs a bullish intraday (5m/15m) tilt
Front price at fair value ($2.911 vs $2.923 fair); managed money net short at the 35th percentile, room to extend
risks: Weather forecasts (8-16D window) can shift quickly, reversing the bullish near-term demand catalyst
Seasonality reliability is Low (59pp 10-90 spread) so the historical pattern may not hold this year
Storage report surprises and COT positioning shifts can move price sharply in either direction
